political.s: B&H Photo is a private company with no publicly disclosed political spending or lobbying activities in federal records.

charity.s: B&H sponsors photography education programs including NYC SALT, Bushwick Stories, and BRIC cultural programming. The company also donated tech equipment to NYC public schools and organized appreciation events for healthcare workers during COVID-19.

environment.s: B&H sells eco-friendly electronics products and promotes green standards like EPEAT and Energy Star. However, the company does not publish comprehensive environmental impact reports or emissions data.

labor.s: B&H has a documented history of employment discrimination. In 2017, the company settled a Department of Labor lawsuit for $3.22 million over allegations of discriminatory hiring, harassment, and unequal pay targeting Hispanic workers. Employee eNPS is negative (-11) and Glassdoor DEI rating is 2.4/5.

dei.s: B&H has a low DEI rating of 2.4 out of 5 stars on Glassdoor, 30.8% below retail sector average. The company faces ongoing complaints about racial and ethnic discrimination in hiring and promotion practices.

animals.s: Not applicable - B&H Photo is an electronics and photography equipment retailer with no animal testing or animal-related policies.

guns.s: Not applicable - B&H Photo does not manufacture or sell firearms; it specializes in photography and electronics equipment.

privacy.s: B&H has a comprehensive privacy policy covering data collection and third-party sharing. The company allows users to opt-out of data sales for targeted advertising. However, PrivacySpy rates the company at 4.7/10, indicating room for improvement in privacy protections.

execPay.s: Not disclosed - B&H Photo is a private company with no publicly available executive compensation data or SEC filings.
